metadata.title: Cisco Pain-Point to Solution Mapping Dictionary
metadata.scope: Public, agent-ready mapping of customer pain language to current Cisco and Splunk solution families. It is not a complete SKU, license, compatibility, or ordering catalog.
design_principles: Store taxonomy as editable data, not hard-coded logic.
Use multi-label matching; one transcript may contain several valid pains.
A literal keyword hit is only a candidate signal.
Require semantic evidence or an independent corroborating signal before notifying.
Explain the exact phrase, cue, evidence, and product-selection rationale.
Distinguish the operational role of each product: observe, assure, orchestrate, enforce, respond, collaborate, or support.
Route uncertain or overlapping cases to a specialist rather than forcing a single product.

sentence_chunking.method: Split by speaker turn and sentence; preserve 1 sentence before and after each candidate sentence.
sentence_chunking.reason: Pain language, negation, ownership, and timing are often distributed across adjacent sentences.

keyword_pass.description: Weighted phrase and keyword match. Longer phrases, product-neutral pain terms, and repeated distinct matches score more than isolated generic words.

semantic_pass.description: Embedding similarity between transcript chunks and semantic cues. Use the maximum match plus a smaller contribution from the next two matches.
semantic_pass.note: Calibrate thresholds on labeled transcripts produced with the exact embedding model used in production.

corroboration_pass.description: CRM, install-base, renewal, opportunity, incident, architecture, or initiative evidence that independently supports the pain and solution.

specificity_and_intent.description: Rewards concrete timing, ownership, quantified impact, active projects, budget, evaluation, renewal, or executive mandate.

penalties.negation: Subtract 0.35 when the customer explicitly denies the pain or says it is already solved.
penalties.hypothetical_or_education: Subtract 0.20 when the statement is clearly hypothetical, training, or generic market discussion.
penalties.wrong_domain: Subtract 0.25 when negative cues indicate a different solution domain.
penalties.competitor_only_context: Subtract 0.10 when a product is mentioned only as an unrelated comparison.

notification_gates.HIGH_INTENT: confidence >= 0.78 AND (semantic >= 0.74 OR corroboration >= 0.70) AND no unresolved negation
notification_gates.NOISE: confidence < 0.62, keyword-only evidence, hypothetical discussion, or explicit negation

transcript_only_mode.maximum_label: REVIEW unless the transcript contains explicit timing, ownership, impact, and buying intent
transcript_only_mode.reason: Without independent corroboration, the agent should not overstate sales intent.

multi_label_policy.selection_rule: Keep categories within 0.08 of the top score when they represent distinct layers; merge duplicates; apply product-role conflict rules.

required_output_schema.why_this_solution: plain-language explanation of product role and fit

customer_language: We have too many network consoles.
There is no unified operational view across campus, branch, and internet.
We need one place to operate the network.
Our teams waste time switching between Meraki, Catalyst, and other tools.
semantic_cues: centralize day-to-day network operations across multiple domains
reduce tool switching between campus, branch, cloud-managed, and internet assurance systems
provide a common operational experience without replacing every underlying controller
negative_cues: single pane specifically for security policy
single pane specifically for collaboration devices
single pane specifically for servers and compute
cross_domain_network_operations.solution: Cisco Networking Platform / Cisco Cloud Control, with Catalyst Center, Meraki Dashboard, and ThousandEyes

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Networking Platform / Cisco Cloud Control
primary_solutions.role: Cross-domain cloud-native network operations layer

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Catalyst Center
primary_solutions.role: Campus and enterprise network automation and assurance

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Meraki Dashboard
primary_solutions.role: Cloud-managed networking operations

primary_solutions.name: Cisco ThousandEyes Assurance
primary_solutions.role: Internet, SaaS, cloud, and end-user assurance

choose_when: The problem spans multiple network domains or management systems.
The buyer is network operations or infrastructure leadership.
The customer wants operational unification rather than a hardware-only refresh.
do_not_choose_when: The problem is primarily SOC alert triage or security policy management.
The customer only needs server lifecycle management.
The issue is isolated to application code or database performance.
corroboration_hints: Existing Catalyst, Meraki, or ThousandEyes footprint
Multiple network-management products in install base
NOC consolidation, tool-reduction, or network-operations transformation initiative

campus_network_assurance.pain_category: Campus network assurance, automation, and troubleshooting
customer_language: Users keep dropping off Wi-Fi and we cannot find the cause.
Provisioning switches takes too long.
We need better client health and network assurance.
Campus changes are manual and inconsistent.
semantic_cues: automate and assure an enterprise campus network
troubleshoot wired and wireless client onboarding or performance
standardize configurations and software lifecycle across Catalyst infrastructure
campus_network_assurance.solution: Cisco Catalyst Center with Catalyst 9000 switching and Cisco Wireless

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Catalyst Center
primary_solutions.role: Campus automation, assurance, topology, client and application health

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Catalyst 9000 switching
primary_solutions.role: Campus access, distribution, and core infrastructure

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Wireless
primary_solutions.role: Enterprise wireless infrastructure

choose_when: The fault domain is the owned campus LAN/WLAN.
The customer needs provisioning, assurance, image management, or client troubleshooting.
Catalyst is present or a campus refresh is planned.
do_not_choose_when: The dominant issue is outside the owned network.
The customer explicitly wants a fully cloud-managed Meraki operating model.
The problem is primarily user identity policy.

internet_saas_assurance.pain_category: Internet, SaaS, cloud, and external dependency visibility
customer_language: The app is slow but our network looks fine.
We cannot tell whether the ISP, SaaS provider, DNS, or cloud is responsible.
We are blind once traffic leaves our network.
Users in one region cannot reach a critical SaaS application.
semantic_cues: isolate performance problems across networks the customer does not own
see hop-by-hop internet and cloud paths
correlate user experience with DNS, BGP, ISP, and SaaS dependencies

primary_solutions.name: Cisco ThousandEyes Assurance
primary_solutions.role: End-to-end assurance across internet, cloud, SaaS, networks, services, and users

choose_when: The suspected fault can be outside the enterprise boundary.
The customer cites ISP, DNS, BGP, SaaS, cloud region, or remote-user path issues.
The goal is fault-domain isolation and experience assurance.
do_not_choose_when: The customer only needs application code instrumentation.
The pain is security alert correlation.
The issue is server provisioning or compute lifecycle.

customer_language: We need to manage hundreds of branches with a small team.
We want zero-touch deployment and one cloud dashboard.
Our distributed sites are inconsistent.
We need simpler networking for stores, clinics, or schools.
semantic_cues: operate many distributed sites with limited local IT
standardize branch networking from a cloud dashboard
simplify deployment of switching, wireless, security, and SD-WAN

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Meraki Dashboard
primary_solutions.role: Cloud management and operational visibility

primary_solutions.name: Meraki MX
primary_solutions.role: Cloud-managed security and SD-WAN

primary_solutions.name: Meraki switching and wireless
primary_solutions.role: Cloud-managed LAN and WLAN

choose_when: The customer values operational simplicity and rapid distributed deployment.
Sites have limited or no local network staff.
A common cloud-managed operating model is a priority.
do_not_choose_when: The customer requires a primarily on-premises controller model.
The environment is a service-provider core.
The principal problem is application telemetry.

enterprise_sdwan.pain_category: Enterprise WAN modernization and application-aware connectivity
customer_language: Our MPLS costs are too high.
We need a more resilient WAN across branches and clouds.
Traffic should take the best path automatically.
We are planning an SD-WAN refresh.
semantic_cues: modernize enterprise WAN connectivity across branches, data centers, and cloud
use application-aware routing and multiple transports
build the networking foundation for SASE
enterprise_sdwan.solution: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN; Meraki SD-WAN for cloud-managed distributed environments

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN
primary_solutions.role: Enterprise SD-WAN with multicloud connectivity, security, and predictive intelligence

primary_solutions.name: Meraki SD-WAN
primary_solutions.role: Cloud-managed SD-WAN for distributed sites

choose_when: WAN transport, branch-to-cloud connectivity, or application path selection is central.
The customer is replacing MPLS or legacy routers.
The customer is building toward SASE.
do_not_choose_when: The need is only user-to-SaaS security without WAN transformation.
The buyer is discussing carrier backbone capacity.
The issue is application code performance.

wireless_refresh.pain_category: Wireless capacity, coverage, reliability, and lifecycle refresh
customer_language: Our Wi-Fi cannot handle device density.
We have dead zones and roaming problems.
We need Wi-Fi 7.
Our access points are aging or out of support.
semantic_cues: increase wireless capacity and reliability for dense or AI-enabled workplaces
refresh aging access points and controllers
improve coverage, roaming, and user experience
wireless_refresh.solution: Cisco Wireless Wi-Fi 7 access points with Catalyst or Meraki management

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Wireless Wi-Fi 7 access points
primary_solutions.role: Modern enterprise WLAN infrastructure

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Catalyst Center or Meraki Dashboard
primary_solutions.role: On-premises or cloud management and assurance

choose_when: The customer names capacity, coverage, roaming, density, or AP lifecycle.
A workplace, campus, venue, healthcare, retail, or education refresh is planned.
do_not_choose_when: The problem is primarily internet/SaaS reachability.
The customer only needs location analytics without a wireless refresh.

smart_spaces.pain_category: Location analytics, occupancy, asset visibility, and smart buildings
customer_language: We do not know how our buildings are being used.
We need occupancy and space-utilization data.
We need to locate devices or assets indoors.
We want the network to act like a sensor.
semantic_cues: turn network and collaboration infrastructure into a source of building intelligence
analyze presence, location, occupancy, or environmental context
connect people, things, and spaces for operational decisions

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Spaces
primary_solutions.role: Cloud platform for location, occupancy, IoT, and smart-space intelligence

choose_when: The use case is physical-space intelligence.
The customer has Cisco wireless, switches, cameras, collaboration devices, or third-party sensors.
do_not_choose_when: The term visibility refers to network or security visibility.
The customer only wants room-device administration.

data_center_networking.pain_category: Data-center fabric modernization, automation, and operations
customer_language: Our data-center network is hard to operate.
We need policy-driven fabric and better segmentation.
Provisioning and troubleshooting Nexus fabrics is too manual.
We need consistent operations across data centers.
semantic_cues: automate and operate data-center network fabrics
apply policy-driven connectivity and segmentation
manage Nexus infrastructure across sites from a central operations platform
data_center_networking.solution: Cisco Nexus Dashboard, Nexus 9000, and Cisco ACI

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Nexus Dashboard
primary_solutions.role: Data-center network operations, automation, lifecycle, and analytics

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Nexus 9000
primary_solutions.role: Data-center switching and AI networking fabric

primary_solutions.name: Cisco ACI
primary_solutions.role: Policy-driven data-center fabric and segmentation

choose_when: The problem is the data-center network fabric.
The customer needs Nexus/ACI operations, automation, visibility, or segmentation.
do_not_choose_when: The customer is asking only for UCS server management.
The issue is distributed branch networking.
The issue is cloud-native application observability.

hybrid_compute_operations.pain_category: Compute infrastructure lifecycle and hybrid operations
customer_language: We need one place to manage our UCS estate.
Server provisioning and firmware compliance are manual.
We need infrastructure lifecycle management from core to edge.
Our compute operations are inconsistent.
semantic_cues: centrally manage Cisco UCS compute infrastructure
automate server, fabric, storage, and provisioning workflows
simplify compute lifecycle from core to edge

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Intersight
primary_solutions.role: Unified intelligent management and lifecycle for Cisco UCS compute

primary_solutions.name: Cisco UCS
primary_solutions.role: Enterprise compute infrastructure

choose_when: The pain centers on servers, compute profiles, firmware, provisioning, or UCS lifecycle.
The customer wants SaaS-delivered infrastructure management.
do_not_choose_when: The pain is primarily network-fabric configuration.
The customer needs application telemetry rather than infrastructure management.

ai_infrastructure.pain_category: Production AI infrastructure, GPU scale, and secure AI factory
customer_language: We need to move AI from pilots into production.
We need GPU infrastructure that can scale.
We need a validated AI stack with security and observability.
Power, cooling, networking, and operational complexity are slowing our AI program.
semantic_cues: deploy a modular, validated enterprise AI infrastructure stack
combine accelerated compute, high-performance networking, security, and observability
scale AI training, fine-tuning, inference, or distributed agents from core to edge
negative_cues: only securing employee use of public AI tools
only monitoring an existing AI application
generic server refresh with no AI workload
ai_infrastructure.solution: Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA and Cisco AI PODs

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA
primary_solutions.role: Full-stack secure AI infrastructure architecture

primary_solutions.name: Cisco AI PODs
primary_solutions.role: Pre-validated modular building blocks for enterprise AI

primary_solutions.name: Cisco UCS and Nexus
primary_solutions.role: Compute and AI networking foundation

primary_solutions.name: Cisco AI Defense and Splunk Observability
primary_solutions.role: AI security and operational visibility

choose_when: The customer is building or modernizing AI infrastructure.
The need spans compute, network, security, observability, and validated design.
do_not_choose_when: The only issue is shadow AI or prompt security.
The customer only needs model/application monitoring.

customer_language: Our SAN is aging.
Storage congestion is affecting critical applications.
We need resilient Fibre Channel connectivity.
We need to modernize mainframe or mission-critical storage networking.
semantic_cues: modernize high-availability storage networking
improve SAN performance, resiliency, security, or extension
operate mission-critical Fibre Channel or mainframe storage fabrics

primary_solutions.name: Cisco MDS 9000 Series
primary_solutions.role: High-performance, resilient, secure SAN switching and directors

primary_solutions.name: Nexus Dashboard SAN management capabilities
primary_solutions.role: Unified SAN operations and insights

choose_when: The customer explicitly references SAN, Fibre Channel, FICON, or storage fabric.
Availability and storage-network performance are business-critical.
do_not_choose_when: The need is general Ethernet switching.
The customer is discussing cloud object storage with no SAN component.
corroboration_hints: Existing MDS or competing SAN estate

industrial_connectivity.pain_category: Industrial connectivity, resilience, and OT network operations
customer_language: We need reliable connectivity in harsh environments.
Factories and field assets are hard to connect and manage.
We need secure remote access to industrial equipment.
Our OT network cannot support automation or industrial AI.
semantic_cues: connect and automate industrial operations in harsh or distributed environments
provide resilient wired, wireless, or cellular connectivity for OT assets
centrally provision and operate industrial edge routers and networks
industrial_connectivity.solution: Cisco Industrial Ethernet, industrial routers and wireless, with IoT Operations Dashboard

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches
primary_solutions.role: Rugged industrial switching

primary_solutions.name: Cisco industrial routers and wireless
primary_solutions.role: Industrial WAN, cellular, and mission-critical wireless connectivity

primary_solutions.name: Cisco IoT Operations Dashboard
primary_solutions.role: Cloud provisioning and management for industrial edge

choose_when: The environment is manufacturing, utilities, transportation, mining, ports, or field operations.
Ruggedization, deterministic uptime, mobility, or remote assets are central.
do_not_choose_when: The pain is a conventional office branch.
The need is exclusively OT threat detection without connectivity modernization.

service_provider_modernization.pain_category: Mass-scale routing, metro/core modernization, and IP-optical convergence
customer_language: We need more core capacity with less power.
Our IP and optical layers are too complex.
We need 400G or 800G scale.
We need to automate a carrier-grade network.
semantic_cues: scale a carrier, webscale, metro, or backbone network
converge IP and optical layers
increase routing density while reducing power and operational complexity
service_provider_modernization.solution: Cisco 8000 Series, Silicon One, Crosswork, and Routed Optical Networking

primary_solutions.name: Cisco 8000 Series Routers
primary_solutions.role: Mass-scale, high-performance routing

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Silicon One and IOS XR
primary_solutions.role: Routing silicon and network operating system

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Crosswork
primary_solutions.role: Service-provider automation and assurance

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Routed Optical Networking
primary_solutions.role: IP-optical convergence with coherent pluggable optics

choose_when: The buyer is a service provider, webscale operator, or very large backbone team.
The need concerns core, peering, metro, DCI, or optical convergence.
do_not_choose_when: The use case is ordinary enterprise SD-WAN.
The customer is refreshing campus access switches.

soc_detection_response.pain_category: SOC alert overload, threat detection, investigation, and response
customer_language: We have too many alerts to triage.
Analysts jump between tools during every incident.
We do not know which threats matter.
Response is too manual and slow.
semantic_cues: correlate security telemetry into prioritized incidents
reduce SOC tool switching and accelerate investigation and response
automate threat detection, investigation, and remediation workflows
soc_detection_response.solution: Cisco XDR and/or Splunk Enterprise Security

primary_solutions.name: Cisco XDR
primary_solutions.role: Curated cross-domain telemetry correlation and prioritized incident response

primary_solutions.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
primary_solutions.role: Broad SIEM/TDIR platform with SOAR, UEBA, detection engineering, and data flexibility

choose_when: Use Cisco XDR when the emphasis is fast correlation and response across endpoint, network, firewall, email, identity, and DNS.
Use Splunk Enterprise Security when the emphasis is broad data onboarding, SIEM, custom detections, compliance, SOAR, and SOC platform consolidation.
Use both when Cisco controls and enterprise-wide data analytics must be joined.
do_not_choose_when: The customer only needs a point firewall or MFA product.
The issue is IT operations alert noise rather than security incidents.
soc_detection_response.recommended_specialist: Security Operations / XDR / Splunk Security specialist

siem_compliance.pain_category: Enterprise security analytics, SIEM, audit, and log-data platform
customer_language: We need centralized logs for security and compliance.
Our SIEM is expensive or hard to scale.
Audit reporting takes too long.
We need custom detections across all our data.
semantic_cues: ingest, search, and analyze security data from many sources at scale
support compliance reporting, threat hunting, and custom detections
consolidate SIEM, SOAR, UEBA, and security analytics
negative_cues: small set of Cisco-native alerts only
siem_compliance.solution: Splunk Enterprise Security on Splunk Cloud Platform or Splunk Enterprise

primary_solutions.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
primary_solutions.role: SIEM and unified threat detection, investigation, and response

primary_solutions.name: Splunk Cloud Platform
primary_solutions.role: Managed cloud data platform

primary_solutions.name: Splunk Enterprise
primary_solutions.role: Customer-managed/on-premises data platform

choose_when: The customer requires broad data flexibility, retention, search, custom analytics, and compliance.
The buying motion is SIEM replacement or SOC data-platform modernization.
do_not_choose_when: The requirement is only simple Cisco-native telemetry correlation.
The problem is IT service health rather than security analytics.

firewall_policy.pain_category: Firewall modernization, segmentation, and distributed policy management
customer_language: Our firewalls are aging.
Policies are inconsistent across sites and clouds.
We need better threat prevention at the edge.
Firewall changes are slow and risky.
semantic_cues: replace or modernize network firewalls
enforce consistent security policy across distributed environments
improve threat inspection, segmentation, and firewall operations
firewall_policy.solution: Cisco Secure Firewall with Cisco Security Cloud Control

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Secure Firewall
primary_solutions.role: Network firewall and threat prevention across branch, campus, data center, and cloud

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Security Cloud Control
primary_solutions.role: Unified cloud-native security and firewall management

choose_when: The customer explicitly references firewall refresh, policy, IPS, perimeter, or segmentation.
Distributed firewall management is a major pain.
do_not_choose_when: The primary need is secure access for users to SaaS/private apps.
The customer only needs workload microsegmentation with no firewall modernization.

identity_zero_trust.pain_category: Identity security, MFA, device trust, and network access control
customer_language: Passwords are our weakest link.
We need phishing-resistant MFA.
We do not know what devices are connecting.

semantic_cues: verify user and device trust before granting access
deploy strong or phishing-resistant authentication
discover and control devices connecting to the network
identity_zero_trust.solution: Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Services Engine

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Duo
primary_solutions.role: Identity security, MFA, access, and device trust

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Identity Services Engine
primary_solutions.role: Network access control, device visibility, segmentation policy, and trust

choose_when: Use Duo for workforce/application authentication and identity access.
Use ISE for network access control, device profiling, and segmentation policy.
Use both for end-to-end user and device trust.
do_not_choose_when: The need is only firewall inspection.
The problem is application performance.

sase_remote_access.pain_category: SSE, SASE, remote access, and branch-to-cloud security
customer_language: Our VPN cannot scale.
Remote users have inconsistent access and security.
We need to secure access to SaaS and private applications.
We are building a SASE architecture.
semantic_cues: provide secure, identity-aware access for users, devices, applications, and agents anywhere

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Secure Access

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN

choose_when: The use case joins connectivity and cloud-delivered security.
Remote users, branches, SaaS, private apps, or VPN replacement are central.
do_not_choose_when: The customer only needs a perimeter firewall refresh.

customer_language: We need better endpoint detection and response.
Malware keeps getting through.
We need threat hunting on endpoints.

semantic_cues: prevent, detect, investigate, and respond to threats on endpoints

do_not_choose_when: The problem is exclusively network flow analytics.
The requirement is only MFA.

network_detection_response.pain_category: Network detection, behavioral analytics, and east-west visibility
customer_language: We cannot see lateral movement.
We need to detect anomalous network behavior.
We want to use network telemetry as a security sensor.

semantic_cues: detect threats by analyzing network behavior and flow telemetry

primary_solutions.name: Cisco XDR

primary_solutions.name: Splunk
primary_solutions.role: Broader analytics and data correlation

choose_when: Network flow behavior is the primary signal.
Agentless coverage or east-west visibility is required.
do_not_choose_when: The need is purely performance monitoring.
The issue is endpoint malware prevention only.

cloud_workload_app_security.pain_category: Hybrid multicloud workload, cloud network, API, and cloud-native application security
customer_language: Security policy is inconsistent across clouds.
We need microsegmentation around workloads.
We do not know our cloud-native application or API risk.
We need protection that follows workloads across data centers and clouds.
semantic_cues: secure workloads and applications across hybrid and multicloud environments
apply distributed segmentation and cloud network policy
find and reduce cloud-native application, Kubernetes, API, posture, and runtime risk
cloud_workload_app_security.solution: Cisco Hypershield, Secure Workload, Multicloud Defense, and Panoptica—selected by control point

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Hypershield
primary_solutions.role: Distributed security enforcement for applications, workloads, and infrastructure

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Secure Workload
primary_solutions.role: Workload visibility and zero-trust microsegmentation

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Multicloud Defense
primary_solutions.role: Cloud-native network security and consistent policy across clouds

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Panoptica
primary_solutions.role: Cloud-native application and API security, including posture and workload protection

choose_when: Use Secure Workload for workload dependency visibility and microsegmentation.
Use Multicloud Defense for cloud network security and policy across VPCs/VNets.
Use Panoptica for cloud-native application, Kubernetes, API, posture, and runtime security.
Use Hypershield for distributed enforcement and segmentation across modern infrastructure.
do_not_choose_when: The customer is asking for cloud-resource orchestration rather than security.
The issue is remote-user access.
The issue is APM or infrastructure monitoring.

ai_security.pain_category: AI application, model, agent, and shadow-AI security
customer_language: Employees are using unsanctioned AI tools.
We need guardrails for our AI applications and agents.
We do not know which models are safe.
Prompt injection and data leakage are major concerns.
semantic_cues: discover and control enterprise use of AI applications

protect AI runtime against prompt, model, agent, and data risks

primary_solutions.name: Cisco AI Defense

do_not_choose_when: The only issue is buying GPUs.

customer_language: We do not know what is connected in our plants.

do_not_choose_when: The environment is ordinary enterprise IT.

phishing_email_dns.pain_category: Phishing, malicious email, DNS, and web threats
customer_language: Phishing is our biggest user risk.
We need to stop malicious links before users click.
We need DNS-layer protection.

semantic_cues: protect users from malicious email, links, domains, and web destinations

hybrid_onprem_apm.pain_category: Hybrid and on-premises application performance linked to business impact
customer_language: We need code-level visibility into a critical application.
Our ERP or Java/.NET application is slow.
We need to connect application performance to business transactions.
Our application estate is hybrid and not fully cloud-native.
semantic_cues: monitor and troubleshoot traditional, hybrid, or on-premises applications
connect code-level application performance to business transactions
find application bottlenecks in mature enterprise application estates

primary_solutions.name: Splunk AppDynamics
primary_solutions.role: Hybrid and on-prem application performance monitoring tied to business performance

choose_when: The estate includes critical traditional, hybrid, or on-prem applications.
Business transactions and code-level application diagnostics are central.
do_not_choose_when: The need is purely internet/SaaS path visibility.
The customer is standardizing exclusively on OpenTelemetry-native cloud monitoring.

service_health_aiops.pain_category: AIOps, alert-noise reduction, service health, and business-impact visibility
customer_language: We have thousands of infrastructure alerts but no service context.
We need to know which technical issue is affecting revenue.
The NOC cannot see dependencies across services.
We need event correlation and probable root cause.
semantic_cues: connect technical telemetry to business-service health
correlate and prioritize IT operations events
show service dependencies, KPIs, business impact, and likely root cause

primary_solutions.name: Splunk IT Service Intelligence
primary_solutions.role: AIOps, service health, event intelligence, and business-impact correlation

choose_when: The buyer needs a manager-of-managers operational view.
Alert noise, service health, dependency mapping, and business impact are central.
do_not_choose_when: The alerts are cybersecurity incidents.
The need is only application instrumentation.

digital_experience.pain_category: End-user and digital experience across application and network layers
customer_language: Customers say the site is slow, but backend metrics look healthy.
We need to test critical user journeys before users complain.
Remote employees have inconsistent application experience.
We need to connect front-end experience to network and backend causes.
semantic_cues: measure real and synthetic user experience
test websites, APIs, and business transactions proactively
correlate front-end, backend, and network causes of poor experience
digital_experience.solution: Splunk RUM/Synthetic Monitoring plus ThousandEyes; AppDynamics where hybrid application context is required

primary_solutions.name: Splunk Real User Monitoring and Synthetic Monitoring
primary_solutions.role: Front-end and proactive user-journey monitoring

primary_solutions.name: Cisco ThousandEyes Assurance
primary_solutions.role: Network, internet, SaaS, DNS, and path assurance

primary_solutions.name: Splunk AppDynamics or Splunk APM
primary_solutions.role: Backend application context

choose_when: The business outcome is end-user experience and multiple fault domains are possible.
User journeys, remote users, websites, APIs, SaaS, or frontend performance are discussed.
do_not_choose_when: The need is only a network hardware refresh.
The issue is security threat detection.

extensible_observability.pain_category: Custom observability solutions and telemetry extensibility
customer_language: We need to build a domain-specific observability solution.
We want an OpenTelemetry-based extensible platform.
Partners need to build custom modules on top of Cisco telemetry.
Our use case does not fit an out-of-box observability product.
semantic_cues: build custom observability applications or extensions
ingest and model metrics, events, logs, and traces on an extensible platform
create partner or industry-specific observability solutions

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Observability Platform
primary_solutions.role: Developer platform for custom OpenTelemetry-based observability solutions

choose_when: The explicit requirement is to build or extend an observability solution.
The buyer is a developer, partner, platform team, or solution builder.
do_not_choose_when: The customer wants an immediately deployable standard APM or ITSI solution.
The term platform is being used loosely for dashboard consolidation.

collaboration_productivity.pain_category: Meetings, messaging, events, and hybrid-work productivity
customer_language: Meetings are fragmented across tools.
Teams need a common collaboration experience.
We want AI summaries, messaging, meetings, and events together.
Hybrid work is inconsistent.
semantic_cues: provide an integrated collaboration suite for meetings, messaging, events, and teamwork
improve hybrid-work productivity and meeting experience
standardize employee collaboration on an enterprise platform

primary_solutions.name: Webex Suite
primary_solutions.role: Integrated collaboration for meetings, messaging, events, and employee experience

choose_when: Employee collaboration is the primary outcome.
The customer wants suite consolidation and AI-enabled teamwork.
do_not_choose_when: The use case is contact-center transformation.
The only requirement is telephony migration.

customer_language: Our PBX is aging.
We need cloud calling across locations.
We want to migrate from on-prem voice at our own pace.
Calling must integrate with Microsoft Teams or Webex.
semantic_cues: modernize enterprise telephony and calling
migrate from on-premises call control to cloud calling
provide enterprise-grade voice integrated with collaboration tools
cloud_calling.solution: Webex Calling, with Cisco Unified Communications Manager for hybrid/on-premises needs

primary_solutions.name: Webex Calling
primary_solutions.role: Cloud enterprise calling and PBX

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Unified Communications Manager
primary_solutions.role: On-premises or hybrid call control

choose_when: Telephony, PBX, PSTN, calling plans, or voice migration are central.
do_not_choose_when: The buyer is discussing customer-service routing and agents.
The need is only meetings.

customer_language: Customers wait too long for support.
Agents switch between too many systems.
We need an omnichannel cloud contact center.
We want AI-assisted customer service.
semantic_cues: modernize customer-service routing, agent workflows, and channels
move contact-center operations to a cloud platform
use AI and analytics to improve customer and agent experience

primary_solutions.name: Webex Contact Center
primary_solutions.role: Cloud contact center and customer-experience platform

primary_solutions.name: Webex Contact Center Enterprise
primary_solutions.role: Feature-rich cloud contact center for large enterprises

choose_when: Agents, queues, IVR, omnichannel service, or customer experience are central.
do_not_choose_when: The use case is internal employee collaboration.
The need is basic enterprise calling only.

room_devices_management.pain_category: Meeting-room devices, workspace readiness, and centralized administration
customer_language: Conference-room devices are hard to manage.
We do not know which rooms are ready or failing.
We need consistent video and audio experiences.
Remote troubleshooting of room systems is too difficult.
semantic_cues: deploy and centrally manage collaboration devices and workspaces
remotely troubleshoot room systems and monitor readiness
standardize meeting-room audio, video, and device experience
room_devices_management.solution: Cisco Collaboration Devices with Webex Control Hub

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Collaboration Devices
primary_solutions.role: Room, desk, board, camera, and peripheral portfolio

primary_solutions.name: Webex Control Hub
primary_solutions.role: Centralized provisioning, administration, analytics, and troubleshooting

choose_when: The pain is physical collaboration spaces or device operations.
Room readiness, device health, deployment, or remote troubleshooting are central.
do_not_choose_when: The need is only occupancy analytics.
The problem is contact-center operations.

lifecycle_services_support.pain_category: Technology lifecycle, adoption, support, and operational readiness
customer_language: We bought the technology but adoption is low.
We need help planning and de-risking the transformation.
We need proactive support and lifecycle insights.
Our team lacks the expertise to deploy and operate this at scale.
semantic_cues: plan, design, deploy, adopt, optimize, and support Cisco technology
reduce transformation risk and accelerate time to value
use proactive insights and expert guidance across the technology lifecycle
negative_cues: specific product capability already clearly identified with no services need
lifecycle_services_support.solution: Cisco Professional Services, Cisco Support, and Cisco IQ

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Professional Services
primary_solutions.role: Planning, design, migration, deployment, and optimization expertise

primary_solutions.name: Cisco Support
primary_solutions.role: Technical support, troubleshooting, and lifecycle assistance

primary_solutions.name: Cisco IQ
primary_solutions.role: AI-powered digital support and lifecycle experience

choose_when: Skills, adoption, migration risk, or operational readiness are blocking value.
The customer requests help beyond product acquisition.
do_not_choose_when: The conversation contains no services, adoption, skills, or lifecycle signal.
